items.title: Social media users in Malaysia must verify age with using IC or passport from June 1, says deputy minister
items.summary: KUCHING, May 22 — Users of social media platforms will need to upload official government-issued documents for age verification following the implementation of two new codes under the Online Safety Act (ONSA) introduced by the Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission (MCMC), effective June 1. “We have now asked platforms to implement age verification. Teo said MCMC, however, had not prescribed any specific technology that platforms must use for the age verification process.

items.title: Johor police arrest six men over fatal slashing of 22‑year‑old in Pekan Nanas
items.summary: ISKANDAR PUTERI, May 22 — Johor police have arrested six local men to assist investigations into the murder of a 22-year-old man believed to have been slashed to death in Pekan Nanas, Pontian yesterday. Acting Johor police chief DCP Hoo Chuan Huat said the suspects were arrested in an operation conducted from midnight until early this morning at several locations around Johor Bahru. In a separate case, police arrested seven foreign men aged between 17 and 45 to assist investigations into the murder of a technician at an apartment in Prima Tebrau, Johor Bahru on May 13.

items.title: Higher Education Ministry: UPUOnline offers places to more than 169,000 SPM leavers for 2026 intake
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, May 22 — A total of 169,803 students who sat for the 2025 Sijil Pelajaran Malaysia (SPM) examination have been offered places at public higher education institutions (IPTA) through UPUOnline. The Ministry of Higher Education (MOHE) said of the total, 91,357 candidates received offers from public universities, 60,947 from polytechnics, 15,844 from community colleges and 1,655 from institutions under Majlis Amanah Rakyat (MARA) Higher Education Division. “A total of 363 programmes were offered for this intake, comprising 41 certificate programmes, 57 foundation programmes, 264 diploma programmes and one bachelor’s degree programme.

items.title: MMEA detains fishing boat for licence breach off Pulau Rimau
items.summary: © New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

items.title: MARKET PULSE PM MAY 22, 2026 [WATCH]
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The FBM KLCI closed higher on Friday as bargain hunting resurfaced following several weak trading sessions. In the cryptocurrency market, Bitcoin fell to RM307,493 as uncertainty over US-Iran negotiations weighed on broader risk sentiment. Following the trend, Ethereum traded lower at RM8,446 while Solana stood at RM347.

items.title: Johor police bust drug lab, seize RM16.4m worth of narcotic‑laced vape cartridges
items.summary: ISKANDAR PUTERI, May 22 — Police busted an illegal drug processing lab that specialised in producing narcotic‑laced vape cartridges worth RM16.4 million were seized in three raids around Skudai and northern Johor Bahru recently. Acting Johor police chief Hoo Chuan Huat said those arrested were two locals and two foreigners. “Police raided premises in Taman Melawati and Taman Ungku Tun Aminah in Skudai that were used as a drug processing laboratory.

items.title: Seafood, water quality in Kuala Juru safe, says Fisheries Department
items.summary: BUKIT MERTAJAM: Laboratory analysis of water and shellfish samples from Kuala Juru waters near here has confirmed that seafood and water quality in the area are safe and comply with national food safety standards. The Fisheries Department said the analysis was conducted by the Fisheries Research Institute (IPP) Batu Maung in collaboration with the Kuantan Fisheries Biosecurity Centre and the Selangor Fisheries Biosecurity Centre, following complaints related to pollution from the local community. The investigation covered heavy metals, water quality, plankton and marine biotoxins.

items.title: JAG back in black with RM5.37mil net profit in Q1
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: JAG Bhd returned to profitability with a net profit of RM5.37 million in the first quarter (Q1) ended March 31, 2026 compared to a net loss of RM6.43 million last year. JAG recorded RM64.58 million revenue, representing a 39.0 per cent increase from RM46.47 million in the corresponding quarter last year. The segment revenue from manufacturing and trading activities increased 41.5 per cent year-on-year (YoY) to RM63.09 million from RM44.60 million.

items.title: Bursa Malaysia ends higher on bargain hunting
items.summary: Turnover rose to 3.68 billion units worth RM3.58 billion compared with 3.49 billion units worth RM3.70 billion on Thursday. Among heavyweights, IHH Healthcare Bhd added four sen to RM8.96, Malayan Banking Bhd and Tenaga Nasional Bhd eased four sen each to RM11.00 and RM14.42, respectively, while Public Bank Bhd and CIMB Group Holdings Bhd were flat at RM4.77 and RM7.75, respectively. On the most active list, SkyeChip Bhd jumped 37 sen to RM2.70, EI Power Bhd rose 7.5 sen to 62.5 sen, Tanco Holdings Bhd put on two sen to RM1.72, Zetrix AI Bhd slid 2.5 sen to 79.5 sen, while GIIB Holdings Bhd was flat at 39.5 sen. © New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

items.title: Only one new abandoned project recorded as delayed, sick private housing cases decline, says deputy housing minister
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, May 22 — Statistics on private housing projects categorised as ‘delayed’ and ‘sick’ in Peninsular Malaysia showed a declining trend, as of April 30 this year, compared with March, said Deputy Housing and Local Government Minister Datuk Aiman Athirah Sabu. She added that, since its establishment in 2023, TFST has successfully revived 1,576 private housing projects, involving 185,525 housing units, with an estimated gross development value (GDV) of RM148.21 billion. Of the total, 131 projects were successfully restored from ‘sick’ to ‘active’ status, 1,410 ‘sick’ projects were fully completed and obtained the Certificate of Completion and Compliance (CCC), and 35 ‘abandoned’ projects were successfully revived, either through physical completion of the projects or via buyer settlement plans.

items.title: Convicted rapist jailed for confining, assaulting teenage boy
items.summary: SHAH ALAM: A convicted rapist today pleaded guilty to physically assaulting and wrongfully confining a 14-year-old boy nearly four hours inside an apartment unit last week. The magistrate's court here sentenced Mohamad Fadwan Abd Muaain, 42, to nine months in jail for confining the teenager by binding his hands and feet, and an additional six months for injuring the teenager. In a separate proceeding at the Sessions Court, Fadwan pleaded guilty to physically assaulting the boy by touching his body and private parts.

items.title: Chery previews flagship Tiggo 9 SUV, estimated price under RM189,000
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Chery Auto Malaysia has previewed Chery Tiggo 9, the largest and most luxurious model in the Tiggo lineup so far, signalling the company's continued push into the premium family SUV segment. Positioned as Chery's flagship SUV, the Tiggo 9 is aimed at families and modern drivers seeking more space, comfort and upscale features without venturing into luxury-brand pricing territory. Chery Auto Malaysia vice president Lee Wen Hsiang said the Tiggo 9 represents an important step in Chery's long-term growth strategy in Malaysia as demand continues to rise for larger and more versatile vehicles.

items.title: Gentari ramps up safety focus at Kedah solar project
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Gentari has reaffirmed its commitment to safety and disciplined project execution as construction progresses on its 150MWp large-scale solar 5 (LSS5) project in Sungai Petani, Kedah. LSS5 is part of Malaysia's broader push to expand renewable energy capacity under the National Energy Transition Roadmap (NETR). "This transition from planning into execution reflects Gentari's commitment to deliver utility-scale renewable energy infrastructure that supports Malaysia's long-term energy transition.

items.title: Fuziah reminds PKR MPs to abide by party decisions, uphold mandate
items.summary: KUANTAN, May 22 — PKR MPs have been reminded to remain committed to the party’s mandate in carrying out their duties as elected representatives. PKR secretary-general Datuk Dr Fuziah Salleh said every elected representative must abide by party decisions and uphold the principles of the struggle entrusted to them. She said several party leaders had been tasked with engaging and advising the MPs involved on their responsibilities in carrying the party’s mandate.

items.title: Massive highway jams as school holiday, festive exodus begin [WATCH]
items.summary: "Three accidents were reported within the space of an hour," the Malaysian Highway Authority (LLM) said in a traffic update on its Whatsapp channel. Minutes later, LLM said that a collision on the Sprint Expressway's Damansara Link eastbound obstructed the left lane near Km2.5. At 4.15pm, it reported that a crash on the East Coast Expressway 1 eastbound near Sri Jaya blocked the emergency lane, though traffic remained manageable.

items.title: Teen killed in crash had just been accepted into polytechnic
items.summary: KOTA BARU: A grieving father said his 18-year-old son, killed in a horrific crash in Kuala Terengganu on Tuesday, had just received news of his acceptance into a polytechnic - a dream he had long waited for. Wah Tee Tun, 49, said the family discovered today that Han Keong had been accepted into Politeknik Sultan Mizan Zainal Abidin to pursue a Diploma in Mechanical Engineering (Automotive). "It stated that my son had been accepted to continue his studies at Politeknik Sultan Mizan Zainal Abidin.

items.title: Construction momentum lifts MRCB's Q1 results
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysian Resources Corp Bhd (MRCB) posted a 46 per cent increase in revenue to RM319.2 million and an 87 per cent growth in pre-tax profit to RM9.1 million in the first quarter of 2026. The stronger performance was driven by improved contributions from the engineering, construction and environment (ECE) division, largely from the recently completed LRT3 project and higher revenues from the Kompleks Sukan Shah Alam redevelopment project. The property development and investment division recorded revenue of RM40.8 million and an operating loss of RM8.9 million in Q1 2026.

items.title: Explosion at MOL's Tiszaujvaros petrochemical plant kills one, injures several
items.summary: Prime Minister Peter Magyar said in a Facebook post that his economy minister Istvan Kapitany and MOL executive chairman Zsolt Hernadi were en route to the plant. "An explosion occurred at MOL Petrochemicals' site in Tiszaujvaros during the restart of the Olefin 1 plant. The Olefin 1 plant is a steam cracker unit at MOL's petrochemical plant in Tiszaujvaros.

items.title: Subang MP raises concern over sudden MyKhas Portal access denial
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Subang member of parliament Wong Chen has alleged that his office was denied access to the MyKhas Portal without prior any notice, thereby affecting applications for allocations for the constituency. He said the restriction has disrupted constituency allocations and Projek Mesra Rakyat applications for Subang residents. The portal, managed by the Implementation Coordination Unit under the Prime Minister's Department, is used to process constituency allocations and Projek Mesra Rakyat applications.

items.title: Domestic Trade Ministry enforces nine‑day Kaamatan‑Gawai price‑control scheme in Sabah, Sarawak and Labuan
items.summary: Armizan said for the 2026 Kaamatan Festival SHMMP in Sabah, a total of 12 types of goods are listed compared to 10 types last year with the addition of imported ginger and potatoes from China. “The items involved are live pigs, pork (meat and fat), pork (belly), imported buffalo meat from India, chicken wings, curly dried chillies, small shallots from India, garlic from China, large yellow onions, large shallots, imported aged ginger and potatoes from China. “For Labuan, 11 types of items are listed, namely pork (meat and fat), pork (belly), imported buffalo meat from India, chicken wings, curly dried chillies, small shallots from India, garlic from China, large yellow onions, large shallots, imported aged ginger and potatoes from China,” he said.

items.title: Lebanon says Israeli strike kills six, including two rescuers and child
items.summary: BEIRUT: An Israeli strike on southern Lebanon killed six people on Friday, including two rescuers and a child, the Lebanese health ministry said, despite a fragile ceasefire in the Israel-Hizbollah war. In a statement, the ministry said that "six people were martyred" including two rescuers from the Risala Scouts association, one of them also working as a freelance photographer, and a Syrian girl in a strike on Deir Qanun al-Nahr village. An earlier strike on the southern town of Hanaway on Friday also killed four rescuers from the Hizbollah-linked Islamic Health Committee, the ministry said.

items.title: Tow truck driver charged with fracturing wife's arm with chair, obstructing policeman
items.summary: MELAKA: A tow truck driver pleaded not guilty at the Ayer Keroh Sessions Court today to a charge of causing grievous hurt to his wife by throwing a vanity table chair at her earlier this month. Muhammad Rizman Akmal Ahmadiah, 28, claimed trial after the charge was read to him before Judge Raja Noor Adilla Raja Mahyaldin. He was accused of causing grievous hurt to his wife, Syaudah Ahmad, 27, by throwing a vanity table chair at her, resulting in a fractured left arm.

items.title: Analysts: Inclusive representation the way forward for Bersama to rival DAP
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Just days after former ministers Datuk Seri Rafizi Ramli and Nik Nazmi Nik Ahmad announced a political "kamikaze" move, the revitalised Parti Bersama Malaysia (Bersama) has emerged as a possible force to reshape Malaysia's political landscape. The recently-acquired party has seen over 8,000 new members in less than 24 hours following its launch on Sunday, according to Rafizi's Instagram announcement on Monday. The party's entry also raises questions about its preparedness to attract voters as election rumours build up.

items.title: LLM forecasts 3.3 million vehicles on highways amid Aidiladha, school holidays
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 22 — The Malaysian Highway Authority (LLM) expects the average daily traffic volume on major expressways and connecting highways to reach 3.3 million vehicles on peak days in conjunction with Hari Raya Aidiladha, which will be celebrated on May 27. It said the PLUS highway network is projected to record the highest traffic volume at 2.2 million vehicles, followed by the Duta-Ulu Kelang Expressway (DUKE) with 283,000 vehicles and the Kuala Lumpur-Karak Expressway (KLK) with 209,000 vehicles. LLM would also be activating 34 Smart Lane locations, including along the PLUS highway network, while ensuring that toll collection systems function efficiently, including staggered toll collection at critical toll plazas.

items.title: Man gets 30 years, 9 strokes for incest with now-pregnant underage niece in Kanowit
items.summary: SIBU, May 22 — The Sessions Court here sentenced a 23-year-old man to a total of 30 years’ jail and nine strokes of the cane for committing incest with his 14-year-old niece. Judge Musyiri Peet imposed a sentence of 10 years’ jail and three strokes of the cane for each of the three charges under Section 376B(1) of the Penal Code, and ordered the imprisonment to run consecutively. The accused was charged with committing incest with his niece on three occasions, namely at around 2am on June 1, 2025 and around 11pm on June 2, 2025, and at around 8.30am on February 13, 2026.

items.title: China squeezes Japan over rare earths in repeat of 2010 showdown
items.summary: SINGAPORE: China has cut Japan off from several heavy rare earths and other materials for at least four months, coinciding with a dispute between the two countries over Taiwan, suggesting Beijing is using its control over critical minerals as diplomatic leverage. Japan is the largest rare earth magnet maker outside China but, like the rest of the world, is overwhelmingly dependent on Beijing for imports of certain so-called heavy rare earths used in magnet-making, aerospace and defence, as well as gallium, a minor metal vital for chip-making. Since December, Chinese exports of rare earth minerals like dysprosium, terbium and yttrium oxide, as well as speciality metal gallium, to Japan have stopped except for a few tiny shipments of yttrium, Chinese customs data shows.

items.title: Viral Bukit Bintang flare stunt ends with fines for clothing brand founder, six others
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 22 — Seven men, including the founder of a local clothing brand, were fined by the magistrates’ courts here today after pleading guilty to a string of offences linked to a viral publicity stunt involving flares and reckless riding in Bukit Bintang last weekend. According to the New Straits Times , the group admitted to various charges before three separate magistrates linked to the early morning stunt at the busy Jalan Sultan Ismail-Jalan Bukit Bintang junction on May 18. Five others — Amir Adzim Shahrul Nizam, 25; Khairul Hamizan Zainudin, 28; Muhammad Izz Mifzal Mohd Noordzlie, 22; Muhammad Ariff Aiman Mohamed Nasir, 23; and Muhammad Ikhwan Daniel Azman, 24 — admitted to committing public nuisance at the so-called “Shibuya Junction” area in Bukit Bintang around 2am that same day.

items.title: Citi to boost Asia wealth business with 'significant' hires, global head says
items.summary: HONG KONG: Citigroup plans to allocate a significant portion of its global wealth management hiring to Asia, where its private bank is growing faster and generating higher productivity than in other regions, the bank's global wealth head Andy Sieg said. The US bank's recently unveiled hiring plans would be "anchored" in Asia along with other regions, said Sieg, who formerly led Merrill Lynch's wealth business and was brought in by Citi CEO Jane Fraser in 2023 to lead a revamp of the wealth unit. "In the private bank, our business in Asia is the fastest growing part of our private bank," Sieg said in an interview in Hong Kong.

items.title: Selangor launches RM20m scheme to ease university fees for state‑born students, says Amirudin
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, May 22 — The Selangor state government has allocated RM20 million to cover half of the tuition fees for 4,000 Selangor-born students pursuing their studies at three state-owned higher education institutions. Selangor Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Amirudin Shari said the three institutions are Universiti Islam Selangor (UIS), Universiti Selangor (UNISEL), and Selangor Technical Skills Development Centre (STDC). He said this at the Selangor People’s Tuition (PTRS) and Didik Kasih (PDK) 2026 programme in Section 7, here today.

items.title: Debt collector jailed 15 months for confining, injuring teenager
items.summary: SHAH ALAM: A debt collector was sentenced to 15 months' jail by the magistrate's vourt today after pleading guilty to wrongfully confining a 14-year-old boy for nearly four hours and injuring him inside an apartment unit last week. The court ordered the divorcee to serve nine months for confining the boy by binding his hands and feet, and an additional six months for injuring the teenager. The court ordered the sentences to run consecutively from today.

items.title: Bank Negara reserves at US$129bil as at May 15
items.summary: According to Bank Negara, the gross international reserves comprise foreign currency reserves (US$113.5 billion), International Monetary Fund (IMF) reserves position (US$1.3 billion), special drawing rights (US$5.9 billion), gold (US$6.4 billion) and other reserve assets (US$2.4 billion). This includes mainly gold, foreign financial assets and other reserves including special drawing rights (RM522.55 billion), Malaysian government papers (RM13.10 billion), deposits with financial institutions (RM1.097 billion), loans and advances (RM28.92 billion), property and equipment (RM4.55 billion) and other assets (RM40.10 billion). Bank Negara papers totalled RM7.59 billion, alongside allocation of special drawing rights (RM26.38 billion) and other liabilities (RM3.76 billion).

items.title: ‘RM300 may seem small, but it’s hard-earned’: Ipoh budget rice stall robbery victim still traumatised
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 22 — A victim of a robbery at a budget rice stall in Lebuh Meru Raya, Ipoh, yesterday said she remains traumatised by the incident. Nor Atheerah Nadzirah Mior Ikram Shah, 21, said she has become easily startled and is now afraid to be alone following the ordeal. She said two men arrived on a motorcycle and stopped at the stall before one of them pretended to place a takeaway food order.

items.title: US navy official says Taiwan arms sales on 'pause' over Iran war
items.summary: WASHINGTON: The acting US Navy secretary said Thursday that arms sales to Taiwan had been put on "pause" to ensure that the American military had sufficient munitions for its Iran operations. Taiwan's presidential office spokeswoman Karen Kuo said Friday there was "no information indicating that the US intends to make any adjustments to this arms sale." A Chinese foreign ministry spokesman told a news conference Friday that Beijing's "position of resolute opposition to US arms sales to China's Taiwan region is consistent, clear and firm."

items.title: MCMC: Failure to comply with Online Safety Act’s risk‑mitigation code may incur fines up to RM10m
items.summary: According to the document, the RMC sets out the steps that must be taken by online service providers under Section 13 of the ONSA to reduce the risk of consumers being exposed to harmful online content on their services. “This includes assessing the risk of harmful content and implementing measures to mitigate that risk, thereby ensuring that their services are safe for users. Under the RMC, the harmful content refers to child sexual abuse material (CSAM), financial fraud, obscene and indecent content, as well as content that may cause harassment or distress.

items.title: Bersatu says issues raised by Hadi will be reviewed at leadership meetings
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 22 — Parti Pribumi Bersatu Malaysia today said it will discuss and scrutinise issues raised by PAS president Tan Sri Abdul Hadi Awang concerning ties between the two parties within Perikatan Nasional (PN). In a brief statement, Bersatu information chief Datuk Tun Faisal Ismail Aziz said the matters raised should be examined comprehensively “in the spirit of camaraderie, mutual understanding and shared responsibility” to strengthen unity within the coalition. Earlier today, Hadi said PAS was reviewing its political relationship and cooperation with Bersatu ahead of the 16th general election, citing several disagreements within Perikatan Nasional.

items.title: Prisons Dept announces special Kaamatan, Gawai visits for inmates' families
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Prisons Department has announced special family visits and meetings for inmates in conjunction with the Kaamatan Festival in Sabah and the Gawai Dayak celebration in Sarawak. The department said the special visits would involve all prison institutions, including Moral Rehabilitation Centres, Special Rehabilitation Centres, Special Detention Facilities and Henry Gurney Schools. "The department said face-to-face visits would be conducted from 8.15am to 4.15pm daily.

items.title: Anwar hails Indah Water's global award for solar-powered sewage project
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said that the international recognition received by Indah Water Konsortium (IWK) reflects the country's commitment to strengthening sustainable water and sanitation management. Anwar, who is also Finance Minister, said that efforts to reinforce this aspect align with the 2030 Sustainable Development Agenda (SDGs). IWK won the Sustainable Development Goal 6 (SDG 6) Award category at the 2026 Global Water Awards in Madrid, Spain, last Tuesday.

items.title: ‘Forgot’ and ‘rarely used’ excuses won’t fly as Penang JPJ seizes RM14m worth of luxury cars
items.summary: BUKIT MERTAJAM, May 22 — The Penang Road Transport Department (JPJ) has seized 51 luxury vehicles worth RM14 million for various offences related to Motor Vehicle Licences (LKM), or road tax, through a special operation dubbed ‘Ops Luxury’ conducted across the state from early January until yesterday. State JPJ director Zulkifly Ismail said a total of 124 actions or offences were recorded against owners of luxury vehicles who failed to renew their respective road tax. “The operation also detected a Ford Mustang that had not renewed its road tax since 2022, with the vehicle’s annual road tax estimated at RM10,900.

items.title: High Court dismisses Mahmud Abu Bekir Taib’s objection against Cahya Mata injunction, citing lack of proper notice
items.summary: KUCHING, May 22 — The High Court here has dismissed Dato Sri Mahmud Abu Bekir Taib’s preliminary objection against an ex parte injunction obtained by Cahya Mata Sarawak Berhad (Cahya Mata) and nine other parties. “No supporting affidavit or affidavit in opposition was filed. The court ordered Bekir to file and serve his affidavit in opposition on or before June 8, while Cahya Mata is to file and serve its affidavit in reply, if any, on or before June 29.

items.title: Bersatu to discuss Hadi's comment on ties with Pas in leadership meetings
items.summary: Bersatu information chief Datuk Tun Faisal Ismail Aziz said the party viewed Hadi's statement on cooperation between Bersatu and Pas within Perikatan Nasional seriously. He said the party would scrutinise every issue raised and discuss them in depth at the Bersatu political bureau meeting and supreme leadership council meeting later. Earlier, Hadi said Pas would re-evaluate its cooperation with Bersatu following several actions by the party that allegedly violated the spirit of camaraderie within PN.

items.title: Police seek man with past murder record over alleged assault, death threats in Ranau
items.summary: RANAU, May 22 — Police are tracking down a 35-year-old man with a previous murder case record following an assault and criminal intimidation incident at a grocery store in Kampung Matupang here on Tuesday. District police chief Supt. Mursalin said the suspect allegedly threatened the victim before punching and kicking him repeatedly, causing injuries to the victim’s lip, shoulder and back, and the victim later sought medical treatment.

items.title: Gate closure improves punctuality but not attendance, says NUTP
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A strict school gate closure policy introduced by a Melaka school may improve punctuality but is unlikely to resolve absenteeism, the National Union of the Teaching Profession (NUTP) said. NUTP secretary-general Fouzi Singon said punctuality and attendance are two separate matters and should not be treated as the same. He said while such measures may encourage discipline and prepare students for working life, they do not necessarily translate into better attendance.

items.title: KLIA taxi tout caught offering RM109 ride to Sungai Long
items.summary: SEPANG: A taxi tout operating at Kuala Lumpur International Airport (KLIA) Terminal 1 was detained by the Road Transport Department (RTD) yesterday. Selangor RTD deputy director Datuk Ahmad Kamarunzaman Mehat said the man had offered transport services using a Toyota Innova, which was subsequently seized. The tout had approached and offered transport services to three Malaysian passengers from KLIA Terminal 1 to Sungai Long, Kajang, for RM109, payable upon arrival.

items.title: Court jails 21‑year‑old man 10 years, six strokes for raping underage girlfriend in Kuching apartment
items.summary: KUCHING, May 22 — A phone salesman who raped his 15-year-old girlfriend has been sentenced to 10 years in prison and six strokes of the cane. Sessions Court Judge Musli Ab Hamid convicted Bong Chou Swee, 21, on his own guilty to a charge under Section 376(1) of the Penal Code for statutory rape, which provides for a maximum 20 years in jail and caning upon conviction. On the night of May 10, the girl sneaked Bong into her apartment, which she later admitted to doing.

items.title: 'Insya-Allah sufficient': PM on Malaysia's energy supply amid Middle East tensions
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has assured that the country's energy supply will remain sufficient despite concerns arising from the ongoing conflict in the Middle East. Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Fadillah Yusof had earlier said the government was working to ensure the country's energy supply could be sustained until December following disruptions from the Middle East conflict. Anwar had previously said Malaysia's energy supply was expected to remain sufficient at least until June.

items.title: Do not normalise remarks seen as belittling Royal Institution, says MHO
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Statements perceived as belittling or questioning the Royal Institution should not be normalised in the country's political discourse. Malaysian International Humanitarian Organisation (MHO) public relations chief Daniel Khoo said all parties, including politicians, should exercise greater caution when making remarks concerning the Malay rulers, as the matter involves public sensitivities and national harmony. He said Malaysians have long witnessed how the Malay rulers consistently care for the welfare of the people and the stability of the country.

items.title: MCMC cracks down on harmful content with two ONSA codes effective June 1
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, May 22 — Two new codes under the Online Safety Act (ONSA) 2025, the Child Protection Code (CPC) and Risk Mitigation Code (RMC), will take effect June 1, requiring digital platforms to take stricter action against harmful content and better protect children online. “These codes set clear expectations for service providers to take greater responsibility in addressing harmful content on their platforms, particularly in protecting children and vulnerable users. Meanwhile, the RMC requires service providers to implement proactive and comprehensive measures to mitigate the risk of harmful content.

items.title: Prison inmates allowed family visits for Hari Raya Aidiladha
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Prison inmates will be allowed special family visits in conjunction with Hari Raya Aidiladha on May 29 and 30. The Prisons Department said the visits, fixed for the third and fourth day of Aidiladha, will involve all prisons, including moral rehabilitation centres, special rehabilitation centres, special detention centres and Henry Gurney schools. The department said visitors are not allowed to bring food or drinks for inmates, adding that these items can be purchased from prison canteens.

items.title: Therapist fined RM4,000 for spreading false Budi Madani RON95 information on Facebook
items.summary: SEPANG: An alternative treatment therapist was fined RM4,000 by the Sessions Court today after pleading guilty to spreading false information regarding the Budi Madani RON95 (Budi95) initiative on Facebook. Judge Ahmad Fuad Othman handed down the sentence to Ariffaizal Mohd Nor Ariffin, 46. He was charged with knowingly creating and sharing a false post via Facebook on April 6 last year with the intent to annoy others.

items.title: ‘Balik kampung’ rush begins at Kuala Lumpur transport hubs as travellers try to beat Aidiladha crowds
items.summary: A Bernama survey found increasing crowds at key terminals, including Terminal Bersepadu Gombak (TBG), Terminal Bersepadu Selatan (TBS) and KL Sentral, as passengers began making early travel preparations to avoid last-minute congestion. A TBG representative, Mohd Raimi Jusoh, said ticket sales for the festive season opened two months ago to give passengers more time to plan their journeys. He said daily ticket sales at TBG, which usually average about 1,000, have surged to around 7,000 during the long holiday and Aidiladha period.

items.title: No other trigger linked to Zara Qairina’s death besides dorm interrogation, inquest told
items.summary: Abdul Fikry: Without the confrontation there is a possibility Zara may have committed suicide due to other triggers or destabilising factors, correct? Abdul Fikry: Before the confrontation, Zara had exhibited predominant suicide risk factors? Dr Chua: Correct.

items.title: Three Myanmar nationals remanded over compatriot’s death in Kepong
items.summary: Sentul deputy police chief Supt Jasni Zolpa said the suspects, aged between 30 and 40, were arrested last Monday. “Initial investigations found that the incident was believed to have stemmed from a misunderstanding that led to a fight between the victim and all the suspects, who were intoxicated, resulting in the victim’s death,” he said at a press conference at the Sentul district police headquarters today. In another development, Jasni said police detained four individuals — one man and three women — to assist in investigations into a fight in Sentul believed to have been caused by personal disputes and debt issues.

items.title: MMEA detains over 4,000 undocumented migrants in first four months of 2026
items.summary: SANDAKAN, May 22 — The Malaysian Maritime Enforcement Agency (MMEA) detained 4,107 illegal immigrants and 316 smugglers nationwide in the first four months of this year. MMEA deputy director-general of Operations Vice Admiral Mohd Zawawi Abdullah said migrant smuggling syndicates remain active and exploit the country’s waters due to Malaysia’s strategic position in the region. Mohd Zawawi said MMEA has opened eight investigation papers related to migrant smuggling cases involving 19 smugglers and 233 illegal immigrants.

items.title: Eighteen-year-old alleged gang leader among six arrested in Sentul motorcycle theft crackdown
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 22 — Police have crippled a motorcycle theft syndicate known as ‘Geng Hadi’ following the arrest of six local men, including its 18-year-old leader, in a series of raids in Kepong last Saturday, leading to the resolution of eight theft cases in Sentul involving estimated losses of RM20,000. Sentul deputy police chief Supt Jasni Zolpa said the suspects, aged between 15 and 33, were detained during an operation conducted by the D4 Branch of the Criminal Investigation Division at the Sentul district police headquarters (IPD). He said during the raids, police seized two motorcycles, five incomplete motorcycle frames, one set of motorcycle components and two mobile phones.

items.title: Mohamad Hasan says Brics offers Malaysia stronger footing in global economy
items.summary: SEREMBAN, May 22 — Malaysia’s participation in Brics is seen as having the potential to strengthen the country’s position in global economic competition, as well as reduce dependence on the traditional financial system dominated by major powers, said Foreign Minister Datuk Seri Mohamad Hasan. Mohamad, who is also Rembau MP and Rantau assemblyman, said Malaysia is now expanding the use of local currencies in trade with neighbouring countries, including Asean members, to reduce reliance on third-party currencies. “Malaysia is a trading nation.

items.title: Prisons Dept: Inmates may receive family visits on May 29–30 for Aidiladha
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 22 — Families of inmates at all prison institutions, including Moral Rehabilitation Centres, Special Rehabilitation Centres, Special Detention Facilities and Henry Gurney Schools, are allowed to visit them for the Aidiladha celebration on the third and fourth days of the festive period. Instead, these may be purchased at the prison canteen,” it said. Families seeking further information may contact the relevant institutions.

items.title: Dissolution of Johor state assembly is Sultan Ibrahim’s absolute prerogative, says Tok Mat
items.summary: SEREMBAN, May 22 — The dissolution of the Johor State Legislative Assembly to pave the way for a state election is the absolute prerogative of the King, His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, according to Barisan Nasional (BN) deputy chairman Datuk Seri Mohamad Hasan. He said the party was confident that Johor Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi would advise Sultan Ibrahim, who is the Sultan of Johor, on the appropriate timing for the state election. On May 16, Onn Hafiz, who is also Johor BN chairman, announced that the coalition would contest all 56 state seats in the upcoming state election.

items.title: Minister urges firm action over alleged sexual grooming of 14‑year‑old boy by female teacher
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, May 22 — The Ministry of Women, Family and Community Development (KPWKM) has called for strict action over a case involving a female teacher alleged to have engaged in sexual grooming and misconduct involving a 14-year-old male student. Its Minister Datuk Seri Nancy Shukri said the case must not be taken lightly and urged the relevant agencies to take firm and immediate action. The ministry under which she is employed should also take firm action,” she said.

items.title: Man charged with Pulau Gaya murder after body found floating near Kampung Lobong
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, May 22 — A 48-year-old man was charged in the Magistrate’s Court here today with the murder of a man whose body was found floating in waters off Kampung Lobong, Pulau Gaya, earlier this month. No plea was recorded from Ali Daham when he appeared before Magistrate Marilyn Kelvin, as the case falls under the jurisdiction of the High Court. Ali was accused of murdering Shazwan Salim at a water village house in Kampung Pondo, Pulau Gaya, at about 9am on May 4.

items.title: Sabah Fire Dept: 72‑year‑old woman found dead after blaze destroys Papar home
items.summary: PAPAR, May 22 — A 72-year-old woman died after a two-storey semi-permanent house caught fire at Kampung Seri Tanjung, Pengalat Kecil, here, this morning. According to a Sabah Fire and Rescue Department spokesperson, a team of seven personnel led by Senior Fire Officer Dahlan Jainudin rushed to the scene after receiving a distress call at 6.29am and arrived at 6.35am at the location, about 4km from the fire station. He said the team later found the unconscious elderly woman in the bathroom area at 7.57am and she was pronounced dead at the scene by medical personnel, believed to have died from breathing difficulties during the fire.
